mnemonics: Schemas are mental frameworks that help organize and structure knowledge
Think of schemas as blueprints for how we think about the world
keyPoints: A schema is a cognitive model that represents a category or concept
Schemas are used to organize and structure knowledge
Schemas can be thought of as mental frameworks or blueprints that help us make sense of the world
Examples of schemas include concepts like 'dog' or 'city'
Schemas can be updated or modified as new information becomes available
The process of creating or updating a schema is called 'schema formation'
